policies.name: Beneficiary registration and payee changes
policies.description: Adding or amending a payment beneficiary is the canonical first step of authorised-push-payment fraud, so registration changes are handed to a human payments specialist for out-of-band verification before any transfer can reference the new payee. The change itself is never hard-blocked: legitimate payee updates are routine and must remain possible.
regulatory_references: HKMA SPM TM-E-1 — Risk Management of E-banking
MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines 2021 s.14 — Online Financial Services

policies.name: High-value corporate payment release
policies.description: Corporate payment releases of SGD/USD 250,000 and above sit outside any single officer's delegated authority and must pass through the documented approval chain, with a named senior manager accountable for the release. An AI agent may prepare the instruction but a human authoriser completes it.
regulatory_references: MAS Guidelines on Individual Accountability and Conduct — Specific Guideline 1

policies.name: Payment recall and SWIFT gpi stop requests
policies.description: Recalling an outgoing payment or invoking a SWIFT gpi stop-and-recall is time-critical and interacts with correspondent banks, so it goes straight to the payments investigations team rather than an automated path. Recall windows close in minutes and a mishandled recall can strand funds at an intermediary.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — SWIFT gpi Stop and Recall (gSRP) service rules

message_contains_any: funds went to the wrong account

policies.name: Standing instruction amendments
policies.description: Amending a standing instruction or GIRO arrangement silently changes where money flows every cycle, so a human approver confirms the customer's mandate before the new schedule takes effect. Unlike one-off transfers, an undetected bad amendment repeats monthly.

policies.name: Vendor payment run release
policies.description: Batch vendor and accounts-payable runs aggregate many invoices into one release, so runs of 100,000 or more require a maker-checker sign-off on the run totals and the vendor master deltas since the last run. Invoice-redirection fraud typically surfaces as a quiet bank-detail change inside an otherwise normal batch.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — maker-checker control on batch payment release

policies.name: Payroll bank file changes
policies.description: Changes to a payroll bank file — new salary accounts, amended amounts, replacement files — are a favourite vector for business email compromise because the file is trusted and runs on a deadline. A payroll control officer re-verifies the change with the originating employer contact through a known channel before the file is accepted.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — dual authorisation of payroll disbursement files

policies.name: Bulk cheque stop payments
policies.description: Stopping a whole cheque series or every outstanding cheque on an account usually signals a lost cheque book or suspected compromise, and it can bounce legitimate in-flight payments. An operations approver confirms the scope of the stop and whether a fraud marker should be set before execution.

message_contains_any: stop payment on the cheque book

policies.name: Card dispute provisional credit
policies.description: Posting provisional credit for a disputed card transaction commits the bank's money before the chargeback is decided, so a disputes officer validates the claim narrative and scheme timeframes first. The agent can assemble the dispute file; the credit itself needs a human approval.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — card scheme chargeback and provisional credit rules

message_contains_any: card charge i did not make

policies.name: Dormant account reactivation
policies.description: Reactivating a dormant account requires a fresh customer due diligence check before transactional capability is restored, because dormant accounts are targeted for takeover precisely when no one is watching them. A human approver confirms identity re-verification is complete before lifting the dormancy flag.
regulatory_references: MAS Notice 626 — Customer Due Diligence (ongoing monitoring)

policies.name: Account closure with funds remittance
policies.description: Closing an account and remitting the residual balance combines an irreversible relationship action with a money movement to a destination that has never received funds before. A human officer verifies the closure mandate and the receiving account ownership before the balance leaves the bank.

message_contains_any: close the account and transfer the balance
close my account and send the money

policies.name: Deceased customer estate settlement
policies.description: Estate claims turn on legal documents — grant of probate, letters of administration, survivorship rules — that an automated agent cannot verify, and releasing funds to the wrong claimant exposes the bank to liability to the rightful estate. All deceased-customer servicing goes to the estates team.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — verification of grant of probate before release of estate funds

policies.name: Account opening and product enquiries
policies.description: Pre-sale enquiries about opening an account, eligibility and published product features involve no customer data changes and no funds, so they stay fully automated. Onboarding itself is governed by the separate CDD policies once an application starts.

policies.name: Term deposit breakage penalty waiver
policies.description: Waiving the penalty on a premature term deposit uplift is a pricing concession that erodes the bank's funding economics and is reserved to deposit supervisors, not the frontline or an agent. The uplift itself can proceed; only the penalty waiver needs sign-off.

policies.name: Credit limit increase beyond delegated authority
policies.description: Limit increases of 25,000 or more sit above standard frontline delegation in most APAC lending authority matrices and must be decided by a credit officer with the right approval grade, against the customer's refreshed serviceability position. The agent may pre-assess but cannot commit the bank's credit exposure.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — delegated lending authority matrices

policies.name: Early loan settlement quotes
policies.description: Producing an early settlement or redemption figure is a read-only calculation from the loan ledger with no funds movement, so it stays automated with a flag for the relationship record. Acting on the quote — receiving the payoff and discharging security — is handled by separate payment policies.

message_contains_any: full settlement figure for my loan

policies.name: Digital loan disbursal to third-party accounts
policies.description: RBI's digital lending framework requires loan proceeds to flow directly into the borrower's own bank account, so any disbursal instruction pointing at an external or third-party destination is pulled out of the automated flow for a lending officer to examine. Pass-through arrangements via lending service providers' pool accounts are specifically what the rule exists to stop.
regulatory_references: RBI Digital Lending Guidelines 2022 — Direct Disbursal of Loan Proceeds

policies.name: Credit decline explanation and review requests
policies.description: When a customer challenges an automated lending decision, FEAT transparency expectations mean the bank owes them a meaningful explanation and a channel for review by someone with authority over the model outcome. The agent must not improvise a rationale for a decision the scorecard made.
regulatory_references: MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Transparency
MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Ethics and Accountability

message_contains_any: credit score used to reject me

policies.name: Overdraft and excess-over-limit authorisation
policies.description: Authorising a temporary overdraft or paying an item into excess is an on-the-spot credit decision against the customer's behaviour score, and it belongs to a credit control officer rather than a conversational agent. Declining or honouring the excess changes the bank's exposure either way.

message_contains_any: honour the payment despite insufficient funds

policies.name: Interest rate adjustments on customer accounts
policies.description: Repricing an individual customer's deposit or lending rate off the published board rate is a discretionary pricing exception that must be approved within the pricing authority grid, and FEAT fairness expectations require that like customers are treated alike. Structured rate-exception requests therefore route to a human approver with the comparable-customer context.
regulatory_references: MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Fairness

policies.name: FX rate overrides
policies.description: Overriding the system FX rate on a customer conversion books an off-market trade against the treasury position and is a classic conduct and P&L-manipulation hotspot, so overrides go to the treasury desk for rate verification and booking. Frontline staff and agents may quote the card rate but never improve it unilaterally.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — FX Global Code Principle 12 (off-market rate transactions)

message_contains_any: better rate than the board rate

policies.name: Treasury dealing limit breaches
policies.description: A breached dealer, counterparty or intraday limit must be escalated to market risk control immediately for ratification or unwind — an agent cannot decide to run an open breach. Limit breaches left to automation are how unauthorised-trading losses compound before anyone looks.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — front office dealing limit governance and breach ratification

policies.name: Trade finance document discrepancies
policies.description: Deciding whether a discrepant presentation under a letter of credit is acceptable is a document-examination judgement under UCP 600 with direct payment consequences for the applicant and issuing bank. A trade finance checker rules on the discrepancy; the agent may only list what it found.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — ICC UCP 600 art. 14 standard for examination of documents

message_contains_any: bill of lading does not match

policies.name: Nostro and suspense break enquiries
policies.description: Questions about unreconciled nostro entries or ageing suspense items are investigative and read-only, so the agent may answer from the reconciliation system while flagging the conversation for the reconciliations team's awareness. Persistent breaks are an early indicator of booking errors or concealment.

policies.name: System cutover during business hours
policies.description: Scheduling a production change, cutover or maintenance window inside business hours risks breaching the tolerance levels set for critical operations such as payments and customer channels, so the request must clear the technology change board with a rollback plan. Out-of-hours windows remain the default the board will push towards.
regulatory_references: APRA CPS 230 — tolerance levels for critical operations
MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines 2021 s.7 — IT Service Management (change management)

policies.name: Disaster recovery and BCP invocation
policies.description: Declaring a disaster, failing over to the DR site or activating the business continuity plan is a crisis-management decision with regulator-notification consequences, reserved to the designated incident commander. An agent surfacing such a request hands over immediately with the triggering context attached.
regulatory_references: APRA CPS 230 — business continuity planning
MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines 2021 s.8 — IT Resilience

policies.name: Core banking production access interdiction
policies.description: Direct agent access to the production core banking database bypasses every application-level control — entitlements, maker-checker, audit journaling — so the request is refused outright rather than escalated. Legitimate data needs are served through governed extracts, never raw production access.
regulatory_references: MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines 2021 s.9 — Access Control
APRA CPS 234 para 21 — information security controls
BNM Risk Management in Technology (RMiT) — Access Control

policies.name: Payment system credential exfiltration interdiction
policies.description: Requests to disclose or transmit SWIFT, RTGS or payment gateway credentials are refused outright: compromised payment-rail credentials are the precondition for the large-scale fraudulent transfer attacks the SWIFT Customer Security Programme exists to prevent. There is no legitimate servicing scenario in which these secrets leave the secure store.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — SWIFT Customer Security Controls Framework
MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines 2021 s.9 — Access Control

policies.name: Bulk customer account data export interdiction
policies.description: Exporting customer account records in bulk through a servicing agent breaches banking secrecy obligations and is indistinguishable from data exfiltration, so the attempt is blocked and logged. Approved reporting pipelines with masking and entitlement checks are the only sanctioned route for bulk data.
regulatory_references: Singapore Banking Act 1970 s.47 — banking secrecy (customer information)
MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines 2021 s.11 — Data and Infrastructure Security

policies.name: E-banking security incident reports
policies.description: Reports that internet or mobile banking is compromised or showing unauthorised logins start the incident clock — including supervisory notification expectations — so they bypass the automated queue and go straight to technology incident response. The agent's only job is rapid, lossless handover of what the customer observed.
regulatory_references: HKMA SPM TM-G-1 — General Principles for Technology Risk Management
MAS Notice on Technology Risk Management — incident notification

policies.name: Routine retail banking credential resets
policies.description: Self-service internet banking password resets that pass the standard identity challenge are the canonical low-risk automation case in retail banking and stay fully automated. Anything touching admin accounts or MFA enrolment is governed by the separate security policies.

policies.name: Overdue KYC periodic refresh actions
policies.description: When a periodic CDD review falls overdue, the consequential servicing actions — restricting the account, chasing documents, or lifting a restriction after refresh — must be confirmed by a CDD officer because the refresh outcome can change the customer's risk rating. The agent may prepare the document checklist but not decide the restriction.
regulatory_references: MAS Notice 626 — Customer Due Diligence (periodic review)

policies.name: Expired identification document flag
policies.description: A customer mentioning an expired passport or identity document during routine servicing is allowed to continue the conversation, but the interaction is flagged so the CDD refresh workflow picks up the stale document before the next gated transaction. No gating is needed for the enquiry itself.

policies.name: Financial hardship assistance requests
policies.description: Hardship and repayment-relief requests involve a vulnerable customer and a credit decision at once: moratoria, restructures and payment holidays must be assessed by a trained officer under the bank's hardship framework, not templated by an agent. Mishandled hardship cases are a recurring conduct finding across APAC supervisors.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — ABA Banking Code of Practice (financial difficulty provisions)

message_contains_any: lost my job and cannot pay

policies.name: Product mis-selling complaints
policies.description: Allegations that a product was mis-sold or unsuitable for the customer's risk profile are conduct matters that feed the bank's complaints and suitability remediation registers, and any response can prejudice the case. A conduct officer owns the substantive reply; FEAT fairness applies where an algorithmic recommendation drove the sale.
regulatory_references: MAS FEAT Principles (2018) — Fairness

message_contains_any: not suitable for my risk profile

policies.name: Fee waivers above frontline authority
policies.description: Waiving more than 250 in fees exceeds the standard frontline goodwill envelope and must be countersigned within the fee-authority grid, with the cumulative waiver history for the customer in view. Repeated just-below-threshold waivers are themselves a leakage pattern reviewers watch for.

policies.name: Small goodwill fee waivers
policies.description: Goodwill waivers under 100 sit inside the delegated frontline envelope and are allowed automatically, with a flag so monthly conduct reporting can spot concentration on any one customer or staff pattern. Keeping small waivers automated protects the human queues for decisions that need them.

policies.name: Relationship-managed pricing concessions
policies.description: Concessions above 5,000 sought by premium and private banking clients are commercially sensitive and precedent-setting for the whole book, so the relationship manager's supervisor approves them rather than the servicing agent. Tier alone never lowers the control bar; it only changes who approves.
